name: Determinism: solo warrior same seed yields identical results
description: Running the solo warrior scenario twice with the same seeds must produce identical scores and game state, confirming engine determinism. If this fails, there is a non-deterministic bug in the engine.

name: Determinism: full balanced party same seed yields identical results
description: Running the full balanced party twice with the same seeds must produce identical results. Tests determinism with multiple hero interactions.

name: Core scenarios with large well-composed parties produce non-negative mean scores
description: Well-composed larger parties (trio+) on normal or easy difficulty should achieve non-negative scores. Small parties (solo/duo) may go negative due to death penalties in late-game scaled encounters. Hard mode and custom penalty modes are excluded because high death rates are expected.

name: Score total equals sum of sub-components for all scenarios
description: For every run, score.total must equal killScore + bossScore + waveScore + speedBonus - deathPenaltyTotal - retreatPenaltyTotal - timePenaltyTotal. A mismatch indicates a scoring rule bug.

name: All scenarios defeat at least one enemy per run
description: Every run should defeat at least one enemy. If a run ends with zero kills, the combat system or spawn logic may be broken.

name: Victory always implies boss was defeated
description: Whenever a run ends in victory, the bossDefeated flag must be true. If victory occurs without boss defeat, the victory condition logic has a bug.

name: Duo party defeats more enemies than solo warrior
description: A warrior+mage duo should kill more enemies than a solo warrior. With enemy scaling, score comparisons across party sizes are unreliable because death penalties scale with party size, but kill counts should still reflect the larger party's superior combat output.

name: Trio party scores higher than duo
description: A 3-hero party (warrior+mage+cleric) should outperform a 2-hero party on average. Validates that adding a healer/third member provides value.

name: Full balanced party defeats more enemies than trio
description: A 6-hero balanced party should kill more enemies than a 3-hero party. Kill counts validate that party size increases combat throughput, even though death penalties may reduce the net score for larger parties.

name: Full party defeats more enemies than solo warrior
description: A 6-hero party should kill more enemies than a solo warrior on average. If not, extra heroes are not contributing to combat effectiveness.

name: Easy mode has higher or equal win rate than normal mode
description: The same party on easy difficulty should win at least as often as on normal. If easy has a lower win rate, the difficulty tuning is inverted.

name: Normal mode has higher or equal win rate than hard mode
description: The same party on normal should win at least as often as on hard. If hard has a higher win rate, difficulty scaling is inverted.

name: Balanced party achieves at least 20% victory rate
description: The balanced 6-hero party should be able to win at least 20% of the time on normal difficulty. A lower rate suggests the game is too punishing for a well-composed party.

name: Solo warrior achieves at least some kills
description: A solo warrior should be able to kill enemies even without a party. Zero kills would indicate the solo experience is completely broken.

name: Balanced party defeats at least as many enemies as mono-warrior party
description: Class diversity should provide at least as much combat effectiveness as stacking the same class. If 4x warriors consistently kill more than a 6-hero balanced team, class balance needs work.

name: Custom mode with max penalties still defeats enemies
description: Even with harsh penalty settings, the party should still be able to defeat enemies. With enemy scaling creating real death penalties, negative scores are expected for punishing configurations.

name: Favourable custom config scores higher than punishing custom config
description: The easy-custom scenario (−50% penalties, +50% exp, −50% difficulty) should score higher than the hard-penalty custom scenario. Validates that custom sliders have the expected directional effect.

name: Mages outperform warriors when enemies resist physical attacks
description: In a world where all enemies resist physical damage, a pure mage party (magical damage) should score higher than a pure warrior party (physical damage). This validates that hero damage type and enemy resistance interact meaningfully.

name: Warriors outperform mages when enemies resist magical attacks
description: In a world where all enemies resist magical damage, a pure warrior party (physical damage) should score higher than a pure mage party (magical damage). This validates that choosing a hero whose damage type avoids enemy resistances matters.

name: All environment scenarios defeat at least one enemy per run
description: Even in unfavorable environments, heroes should still be able to defeat enemies.

name: Favorably-matched environment scenarios produce non-negative mean scores
description: Heroes in a neutral world or facing only the resistance they are resistant to should achieve non-negative scores. Mismatched parties (e.g., warriors in a physical-resist world) may go negative, which is expected and validates that resistance creates meaningful consequences.

name: All new environment scenarios defeat at least one enemy per run
description: Even in unfavorable environments, heroes should still be able to defeat enemies.

name: Mages significantly outperform warriors in physical-resist world
description: In a 100% physical resistance world, mages (magical damage) should score at least 5% more than warriors (physical damage). A tiny margin suggests resistances are not impactful enough.

name: Warriors significantly outperform mages in magical-resist world
description: In a 100% magical resistance world, warriors (physical damage) should score at least 5% more than mages (magical damage).

name: Warriors in neutral world outperform warriors in physical-resist world
description: Warriors should score higher in a neutral world (no enemy resistances) than in a world where all enemies resist physical attacks. Validates that environmental resistance degrades performance of mismatched heroes.

name: Mages in neutral world outperform mages in magical-resist world
description: Mages should score higher in a neutral world than in a world where all enemies resist magical attacks.

name: Mages underperform in fire-resist world compared to neutral
description: Mages deal fire-element damage. In a 100% fire-resist world, their element is penalised, so they should score lower than in a neutral world.

name: Paladins underperform in light-resist world compared to neutral
description: Paladins deal light-element damage. In a 100% light-resist world, their element is penalised, so they should score lower than in a neutral world.

name: Mages still outperform warriors in physical+fire resist world
description: In a world with both physical and fire resistance, mages (magical+fire) lose their element advantage but still bypass physical resistance. Warriors (physical+neutral) are fully penalised by physical resistance. Mages should still score higher.
